# mhost — AI-Powered Process Manager

> mhost is a process manager written in Rust that replaces PM2. Single binary, zero dependencies, cross-platform.

## Key Facts
- Language: Rust
- License: MIT (free, open source)
- Platforms: macOS, Linux, Windows
- Install: npm, Homebrew, Cargo, curl, Docker
- Website: https://mhostai.com
- GitHub: https://github.com/maqalaqil/mhost
- npm: @maqalaqil93/mhost

## What It Does
- Process management: start, stop, restart, scale, cluster
- Health probes: HTTP, TCP, script-based
- Notifications: Telegram, Slack, Discord, Email, PagerDuty, Teams, Ntfy, Webhook
- Log engine: FTS5 full-text search, JSON auto-parsing, 4 external sinks
- Metrics: Prometheus export, alerts, auto-remediation
- Reverse proxy: HTTP/HTTPS, auto-TLS, load balancing
- Deploy engine: git pull, hooks, rollback
- AI: 10 commands (diagnose, optimize, config generation, log queries, postmortem)
- Autonomous agent: monitors 24/7, communicates via Telegram, 3 autonomy levels
- Self-healing brain: persistent memory, 8 playbooks, health scores, auto-learning
- Cloud fleet: manage EC2, Azure, DigitalOcean, Railway via SSH
- Chat bot: Telegram/Discord control with role-based permissions
- Web dashboard: real-time browser UI
- TUI: terminal dashboard with sparklines
- Dev mode: file watcher, auto-restart, .env loading

## Install
```
curl -fsSL mhostai.com/install.sh | sh
npm install -g @maqalaqil93/mhost
brew install maqalaqil/tap/mhost
cargo install mhost
```

## Quick Start
```
mhost start server.js          # Auto-detects Node.js
mhost list                     # See processes
mhost logs api --follow        # Live log tail
mhost dashboard                # Web UI
mhost ai diagnose api          # AI crash analysis
mhost agent start              # Autonomous agent
```

## Documentation
https://mhostai.com/docs.html
